edges.description: Disrupted neurogenesis and developmental GABA signaling manifest as global developmental delay.

edges.description: Disrupted neurodevelopment manifests as intellectual disability.

edges.description: Neurodevelopmental impairment manifests as delayed, poor, or absent speech.

edges.description: Neurodevelopmental motor involvement manifests as hypotonia and delayed walking.

edges.description: Impaired inner-ear ion transport and endolymph homeostasis manifest as bilateral sensorineural hearing loss.
Inner-ear SLC12A2 dysfunction can involve vestibular function and manifest as vestibular areflexia.

nodes.description: Autistic spectrum features including social communication difficulties and restricted interests are observed in some affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: De Novo SLC12A2 Variant Presenting as Congenital Hearing Loss With Vestibular Areflexia.
evidence.snippet: de novo heterozygous SLC12A2 variants have also been implicated in developmental delay with autistic features.

nodes.description: Sensorineural deafness occurs in a subset of patients due to impaired endolymph production from NKCC1 dysfunction in the stria vascularis.

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: Two had sensorineural deafness. We also identified SLC12A2 variants in three individuals with non-syndromic bilateral sensorineural hearing loss and vestibular areflexia.

evidence.reference_title: De Novo SLC12A2 Variant Presenting as Congenital Hearing Loss With Vestibular Areflexia.
evidence.snippet: We report a patient with profound congenital hearing loss and vestibular areflexia with a de novo variant in SLC12A2 located in the splice donor site: NM_001046.2: c.2977+4_2977+7del causing an in-frame skip of exon 21

nodes.description: Structural brain malformation reflecting disrupted neuronal migration during fetal development, observed on brain MRI in some patients.

evidence.snippet: brain anomalies (including cortical dysplasia and agenesis of the corpus callosum)

nodes.description: Speech and language development is significantly delayed or absent.

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: All had developmental delay or intellectual disability ranging from mild to severe.

nodes.description: Delayed attainment of developmental milestones is a hallmark feature, with all identified patients showing motor and cognitive delays from infancy.

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: All had developmental delay or intellectual disability ranging from mild to severe.

nodes.description: NKCC1 plays a critical role in establishing intracellular chloride concentration in developing neurons. In radial glial cells during fetal corticogenesis (16-18 weeks post-conception), NKCC1 maintains elevated intracellular chloride, making GABA depolarizing and excitatory, which supports neuronal migration, differentiation, and synaptogenesis. Mutations disrupting NKCC1 function prematurely shift the developmental GABA excitatory-inhibitory switch, impairing neural progenitor expansion and cortical development.
nodes.id: Impaired Neurogenesis via GABAergic Signaling Disruption

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: Analysis of SLC12A2 expression in foetal brain at 16-18 weeks post-conception revealed high expression in radial glial cells, compatible with a role in neurogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: Gene co-expression analysis in cells robustly expressing SLC12A2 at 16-18 weeks post-conception identified a transcriptomic programme associated with active neurogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Abnormal GABAA receptor-mediated currents in dorsal root ganglion neurons isolated from Na-K-2Cl cotransporter null mice.
evidence.snippet: In contrast to control neurons, little or no net current was observed at the resting membrane potential in homozygote NKCC1 mutant DRG neurons. E(GABA) was significantly more negative, demonstrating the absence of Cl(-) accumulation in these cells.

nodes.description: NKCC1 is essential for endolymph production in the inner ear. It is expressed in the strial marginal cells of the cochlea and vestibular dark cells, where it drives potassium and chloride transport into the endolymph. Loss of NKCC1 function leads to reduced endolymph volume, decreased endocochlear potential, hair cell degeneration, and bilateral sensorineural deafness with vestibular areflexia.

evidence.reference_title: Nkcc1 (Slc12a2) is required for the regulation of endolymph volume in the otic vesicle and swim bladder volume in the zebrafish larva.
evidence.snippet: We show here that lte mutations correspond to lesions in nkcc1.

evidence.reference_title: De Novo SLC12A2 Variant Presenting as Congenital Hearing Loss With Vestibular Areflexia.
evidence.snippet: In the inner ear, K+ transport is required for the mechano-transduction of auditory stimuli. SLC12A2 functions as a dimer and has several isoforms; only one isoform contains exon 21, and this isoform is almost exclusively expressed in the inner ear/cochlea.

nodes.description: Mild to moderate intellectual disability is present in most affected individuals, with some cases showing severe impairment.

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: All had developmental delay or intellectual disability ranging from mild to severe.

nodes.description: Missense mutations affecting evolutionarily conserved residues including p.(Ala327Val), p.(Arg410Gln), p.(Asn376Ile), p.(Ala379Pro), and p.(Glu980Lys).
Reduced muscle tone is commonly observed in infancy, contributing to delayed motor milestones. Some patients transition from hypotonia to hypertonia and spasticity.

evidence.snippet: hypotonia with delayed or absent walking

nodes.description: Frameshift and nonsense variants including p.(Val1026Phefs*2) from an 11-bp deletion in exon 22. GnomAD shows strong constraint (pLI = 0.96).

Delpire-McNeill syndrome is caused by de novo heterozygous mutations in the SLC12A2 gene, which encodes the Na-K-2Cl cotransporter 1 (NKCC1). NKCC1 is a widely expressed electroneutral cation-chloride cotransporter that mediates coupled transport of sodium, potassium, and chloride ions across cell membranes. Loss-of-function mutations disrupt intracellular chloride homeostasis, affecting neuronal development, auditory function, and multiple organ systems. All tested variants reduce cotransporter function in Xenopus laevis oocytes.

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: Through trio exome sequencing we identified de novo mutations in SLC12A2 in six children with neurodevelopmental disorders. All had developmental delay or intellectual disability ranging from mild to severe.

evidence.reference_title: NKCC1 in human diseases: is the SLC12A2 gene haploinsufficient?
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the SLC12A2 gene, which encodes the Na-K-2Cl cotransporter-1 (NKCC1), are linked to various conditions such as neurodevelopmental deficits, deafness, and fluid secretion in different epithelia.

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: The SLC12A2 de novo mutation rate was demonstrated to be significantly elevated in the deciphering developmental disorders cohort.

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: All tested variants were shown to reduce co-transporter function in Xenopus laevis oocytes.

evidence.reference_title: A patient with multisystem dysfunction carries a truncation mutation in human SLC12A2, the gene encoding the Na-K-2Cl cotransporter, NKCC1.
evidence.snippet: Exome sequencing revealed a de novo, loss-of-function allele in SLC12A2, the gene encoding the Na-K-2Cl cotransporter-1. The 11-bp deletion in exon 22 results in frameshift (p.Val1026Phefs*2) and truncation of the carboxy-terminal tail of the cotransporter.

nodes.description: NKCC1 is expressed on basolateral membranes of secretory epithelia including salivary glands, pancreas, trachea, intestine, and sweat glands, where it supports transepithelial fluid secretion. Severe NKCC1 deficiency causes absent or reduced salivation, tear production, and sweat production, as well as respiratory complications from dry mucosa and mucus plugging.

evidence.reference_title: A patient with multisystem dysfunction carries a truncation mutation in human SLC12A2, the gene encoding the Na-K-2Cl cotransporter, NKCC1.
evidence.snippet: This study describes a 13-yr-old girl with orthostatic intolerance, respiratory weakness, multiple endocrine abnormalities, pancreatic insufficiency, and multiorgan failure involving the gut and bladder.

evidence.reference_title: Kilquist syndrome: A novel syndromic hearing loss disorder caused by homozygous deletion of SLC12A2.
evidence.snippet: Herein we describe a child admitted to the NIH Undiagnosed Diseases Program with global developmental delay, sensorineural hearing loss, gastrointestinal abnormalities, and absent salivation.

nodes.description: Some patients transition from early hypotonia to hypertonia and spasticity.

evidence.snippet: brain anomalies (including cortical dysplasia and agenesis of the corpus callosum) and spasticity

nodes.description: Bilateral loss of vestibular function frequently accompanies hearing impairment, contributing to delayed motor development and balance difficulties.

evidence.reference_title: SLC12A2 variants cause a neurodevelopmental disorder or cochleovestibular defect.
evidence.snippet: Through trio exome sequencing we identified de novo mutations in SLC12A2 in six children with neurodevelopmental disorders. All had developmental delay or intellectual disability ranging from mild to severe. Two had sensorineural deafness. We also identified SLC12A2 variants in three individuals with non-syndromic bilateral sensorineural hearing loss and vestibular areflexia.

evidence.reference_title: De Novo SLC12A2 Variant Presenting as Congenital Hearing Loss With Vestibular Areflexia.
evidence.snippet: We report a patient with profound congenital hearing loss and vestibular areflexia with a de novo variant in SLC12A2
